meta.section: M365 Admin Center — Self-Service Trials & Purchases
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-365/commerce) — MSCommerce module cmdlets, admin-center path, and per-product ProductIds grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: All 27 rows in this section use ONE mechanism: the AllowSelfServicePurchase policy, set per-product to Disabled. Guidance therefore repeats the same workflow for each product, differing only by product name and ProductId.

AllowSelfServicePurchase controls BOTH self-service purchases and trials for a product. Setting a product to "Do not allow" (Disabled) stops users from buying or starting a trial with their own payment method; admins keep full ability to assign licenses centrally.

There is no tenant-wide off switch — the whole point of this section is to disable each product one at a time. New products Microsoft adds default to Allowed, so treat this as a recurring verification, not a set-once task.

ProductIds occasionally change and some products get renamed (e.g. Clipchamp Premium, Planner Plan 1). When in doubt, run Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase to see the live ProductName/ProductId/PolicyValue before setting anything.

requiredSettings.label: AllowSelfServicePurchase
requiredSettings.value: Disabled — blocks both self-service purchase AND trial sign-up for Dynamics 365 Marketing (admin can still assign licenses centrally).

requiredSettings.label: ProductId
requiredSettings.value: CFQ7TTC0LH3N

requiredSettings.label: Admin role
requiredSettings.value: Global Administrator or Billing Administrator to change; Global Reader for read-only.

steps: Admin-center method (quickest for one product): sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center (admin.microsoft.com) as a Global or Billing Administrator.
Go to Settings > Org settings > Services tab > Self-service trials and purchases. (Older tenants label this 'User-owned apps and services'.)
Select Dynamics 365 Marketing in the list, set it to 'Do not allow', and Save changes.

PowerShell (bulk-friendly): install once with Install-Module -Name MSCommerce, then each session run Import-Module MSCommerce and Connect-MSCommerce (sign in as Global/Billing admin). List products: Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase.

Disable it: Update-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId CFQ7TTC0LH3N -Value "Disabled". (Use "OnlyTrialsWithoutPaymentMethod" instead only if no-payment trials are acceptable.)
Verify: Get-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId CFQ7TTC0LH3N — expect PolicyValue = Disabled. In the admin center the product now reads "Do not allow".

Note: AllowSelfServicePurchase is forward-looking (existing user purchases/trials aren't revoked) and can take up to 72 hours to apply. There is no single tenant-wide switch — each product is set individually, and newly added products default to Allowed, so re-check this list periodically.

requiredSettings.value: Not in Microsoft's published sample list — read the current ProductId from the Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies output (step 4).

requiredSettings.label: Admin role
requiredSettings.value: Global Administrator or Billing Administrator to change; Global Reader for read-only.

steps: Admin-center method (quickest for one product): sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center (admin.microsoft.com) as a Global or Billing Administrator.
Go to Settings > Org settings > Services tab > Self-service trials and purchases. (Older tenants label this 'User-owned apps and services'.)

PowerShell (bulk-friendly): install once with Install-Module -Name MSCommerce, then each session run Import-Module MSCommerce and Connect-MSCommerce (sign in as Global/Billing admin). List products: Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase.
Disable it: Update-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId <ProductId-from-step-4> -Value "Disabled".
Verify: re-run Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase and confirm this product shows PolicyValue = Disabled (admin center reads "Do not allow").

Note: AllowSelfServicePurchase is forward-looking (existing user purchases/trials aren't revoked) and can take up to 72 hours to apply. There is no single tenant-wide switch — each product is set individually, and newly added products default to Allowed, so re-check this list periodically.

steps: Admin-center method (quickest for one product): sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center (admin.microsoft.com) as a Global or Billing Administrator.
Go to Settings > Org settings > Services tab > Self-service trials and purchases. (Older tenants label this 'User-owned apps and services'.)
Select Teams Essentials in the list, set it to 'Do not allow', and Save changes.

PowerShell (bulk-friendly): install once with Install-Module -Name MSCommerce, then each session run Import-Module MSCommerce and Connect-MSCommerce (sign in as Global/Billing admin). List products: Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase. Heads-up: this product appears in the list as "Teams Essential", not "Teams Essentials" — match on that.

Disable it: Update-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId CFQ7TTC0JN4R -Value "Disabled". (Use "OnlyTrialsWithoutPaymentMethod" instead only if no-payment trials are acceptable.)
Verify: Get-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId CFQ7TTC0JN4R — expect PolicyValue = Disabled. In the admin center the product now reads "Do not allow".

Note: AllowSelfServicePurchase is forward-looking (existing user purchases/trials aren't revoked) and can take up to 72 hours to apply. There is no single tenant-wide switch — each product is set individually, and newly added products default to Allowed, so re-check this list periodically.
